exchange_b.name: OKX
weaknesses: Lower volume on alt pairs vs Binance

rate_mechanics: Both exchanges use 8-hour funding intervals with TWAP-based rate calculations. Bybit's funding rates tend to be more volatile due to higher retail participation, while OKX rates are generally more stable with institutional order flow. OKX offers portfolio margin which can reduce funding costs through cross-margining, whereas Bybit's unified account provides simpler position management. Both exchanges cap funding rates at ±2.5% per interval during extreme market conditions.
verdict: OKX edges out with slightly lower taker fees and portfolio margin benefits for large traders. Bybit excels for pure derivatives trading with faster execution and simpler fee structures for retail arbitrageurs.

faq.question: Which exchange has better funding rates for arbitrage?
faq.answer: OKX generally offers more stable funding rates due to institutional flow, while Bybit's rates are more volatile but can provide better opportunities during market stress. Both use identical 8-hour intervals making cross-exchange arbitrage feasible.

faq.question: How do the margin requirements compare between Bybit and OKX?
faq.answer: OKX's portfolio margin mode allows cross-margining between spot and derivatives, reducing overall margin requirements. Bybit uses isolated and cross margin but doesn't offer portfolio margining, requiring higher individual position margins.

faq.question: Which platform has better API connectivity for automated funding arbitrage?
faq.answer: Both offer robust APIs, but OKX has lower latency and higher rate limits for institutional clients. Bybit's API is more straightforward for retail developers with better documentation and simpler authentication.

faq.question: Are there differences in funding rate calculation methods?
faq.answer: Both use similar TWAP-based calculations referencing spot prices, but OKX incorporates more diverse spot markets in its index. Bybit's calculation is more transparent but can be influenced by its own spot market during low liquidity periods.

seo.title: Bybit vs OKX Funding Rates: Perpetual Futures Comparison
seo.description: Compare Bybit and OKX perpetual futures funding rates, fees, and arbitrage opportunities. Analysis of 8-hour intervals, margin requirements, and API capabilities.
